description: Fix 7 bugs identified in the v3 code review: adversarial filter dead code, tick data reader/writer mismatch, Kelly sizing attribute error, Monte Carlo static balance, adversarial filter dir() scope, spread monitor race (minor/acceptable), and correlation regime data pipeline (add SPY/TNX to fetch).
steps: Fix adversarial filter in signal_generator.py: Replace `direction=action` with `direction=momentum_direction` since action is not yet defined at that point
Fix adversarial filter dir() scope check in signal_generator.py: Replace `regime_name if 'regime_name' in dir() else 'unknown'` with a proper default variable approach
Fix tick data reader/writer mismatch in tick_data.py: Change poll_tick_file() to read the entire file each time (since EA rewrites the full buffer), replacing the incremental seek-based approach
Fix Kelly sizing attribute error in signal_generator.py: Replace `self.risk_manager._account_balance` with `self.risk_manager._current_equity`
Fix Monte Carlo static account balance: Add account_balance parameter to update_state() and wire live equity from main.py trade confirmation callback
Fix correlation regime data pipeline: Add SPY and TNX to MultiPairConfig default pairs and yfinance_tickers so they are fetched and available for the CorrelationRegimeDetector
acceptance_criteria: Adversarial filter uses momentum_direction (not undefined action) for direction parameter
Adversarial filter uses a pre-initialized variable for regime, not dir() scope check
Tick data processor reads entire file on each poll (no seek-based incremental reads)
Kelly sizing accesses self.risk_manager._current_equity for account balance
Monte Carlo update_state() accepts and uses live account_balance from equity tracking
MultiPairConfig includes SPY and TNX in the default pairs and yfinance_tickers
All Python imports still work cleanly
verification: Run: cd python_bridge_v3 && python3 -c "from main import PythonMLBridge; print('Full v3 import OK')"
Run: cd python_bridge_v3 && python3 -c "from data.tick_data import TickDataProcessor; t = TickDataProcessor(); print('Tick data OK')"

Run: cd python_bridge_v3 && python3 -c "from strategies.monte_carlo import MonteCarloRiskSimulator; m = MonteCarloRiskSimulator(); m.update_state(drawdown=0.01, account_balance=50000); print('MC update OK')"

Run: cd python_bridge_v3 && python3 -c "from config.settings import MultiPairConfig; c = MultiPairConfig(); assert 'SPY' in c.yfinance_tickers or 'spy' in [p.lower() for p in c.pairs]; print('MultiPair has SPY')"
findings: All 6 actionable bugs fixed and verified. Issue #6 (spread monitor historical lines logic) was noted as minor/acceptable in the review - the design works correctly with sequential polls building the buffer over time. Issue #7 (correlation regime data pipeline) was already partially wired in main.py (the code looks for DXY/SPY/TNX keys in pair_data); the fix adds SPY and TNX to the default MultiPairConfig so they actually get fetched. The Monte Carlo now accepts live account_balance via update_state() and main.py passes it from the risk_manager on each trade close. All imports verified clean.
